file_name: Zounds Hearing, Inc. - MANUFACTURING DESIGN MARKETING AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: Manufacturer represents that all sales to Manufacturer under this Agreement are sales for use in production or resale; therefore no sales, use, excise or other taxes are due as a result of such sales and Manufacturer will be responsible for payment of any such taxes. A copy of the Manufacturer's resale certificate will be provided by Manufacturer within 15-days of the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: Manufacturer represents that all sales to Manufacturer under this Agreement are sales for use in production or resale; therefore Manufacturer will be responsible for payment of any such taxes. A copy of the Manufacturer's resale certificate will be provided by Manufacturer within 15-days of the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'therefore no sales, use, excise or other taxes are due as a result of such sales' has been removed. This creates an in-text contradiction because the Manufacturer is stated to be responsible for payment of such taxes, while the deleted clause implies they shouldn't be. This is contradictory and creates uncertainty regarding tax liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon Subcontractor's approval and acceptance of a production forecast provided by Manufacturer, Manufacturer's payment for Manufacturer's Products will be due when forecasted materials or components must be ordered by the Subcontractor as set forth in Section 3 above. A schedule of the forecast payables will be provided by the Subcontractor to the Manufacturer based on the approved forecast. Components and products will only be procured and/or manufactured once payment has been received. All payments will be made by electronic wire transfer and all of the appropriate forms will need to be signed so that this can happen efficiently. Alternately, payments can be made to Subcontractor's address as shown Subcontractor's invoice. No offsets are allowed to be taken by either Party. Manufacturer represents that all sales to Manufacturer under this Agreement are sales for use in production or resale; therefore Manufacturer will be responsible for payment of any such taxes. A copy of the Manufacturer's resale certificate will be provided by Manufacturer within 15-days of the Effective Date.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that no sales, use, excise, or other taxes are due as a result of sales under the agreement. The changed text removes this clause, while still stating that the Manufacturer is responsible for payment of any such taxes. This creates a direct contradiction because the original text implied an exemption from these taxes, which is now removed, directly contradicting the original terms. This aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction, as essential language has been removed creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated elsewhere in the document. The change creates uncertainty regarding tax liability.

perturbation.original_text: Royalties shall be paid 50% at time of shipping and the remaining balance of 50% due in 15-days after the Manufacturer's Products have shipped from the manufacturing facility.
perturbation.changed_text: Royalties shall be paid at time of shipping.
perturbation.explanation: The clause specifying that the remaining balance of 50% is due 15 days after shipment is removed. Section 3.5 is contradicted because previously there was a specific timeline for payments (50% upfront, 50% within 15 days) but now there is no clarification about when the royalties are paid. This ambiguity causes confusion and creates uncertainty regarding the payment terms, contradicting any implied understanding of installment payments for royalties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In addition to paying the Product Costs of Manufacturer's Products, Manufacturer shall also pay to Subcontractor the following royalty payments (each a "Royalty" and collectively the "Royalties") for each of Manufacturer's Product purchased under this Agreement. Royalties shall be paid at time of shipping. a) Non-Rechargeable Products. For Manufacturer's Products that are non-rechargeable, Manufacturer shall pay Subcontractor a Royalty equal to the higher of (i) eighty percent (80.0%) of the Product Cost; and (ii) $80 per unit. b) Rechargeable Products. For Manufacturer's Products that are rechargeable, Manufacturer shall pay Subcontractor a Royalty equal to the higher of (i) one hundred percent (100.0%) of the Product Cost; and (ii) $100 per unit.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a payment schedule of 50% at shipping and 50% within 15 days after. The changed text removes the 15-day payment provision, now stating that royalties are paid "at time of shipping". This directly contradicts the expectation of installment payments implied in the contradicted section. The contradiction arises because the changed text introduces ambiguity regarding payment timelines, while the contradicted section establishes royalty payments based on the original, more specific timeline, and is now only referencing 'at the time of shipping', causing confusion and potential disputes over payment obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Manufacturer agrees and acknowledges that all shipment dates are firm delivery dates.
perturbation.changed_text: Manufacturer agrees and acknowledges that all shipment dates are projected delivery dates.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'firm delivery dates' is replaced with 'projected delivery dates'. This introduces a contradiction because the term 'firm delivery dates' means that it is expected to be guaranteed, where as the term 'projected delivery dates' means that it is expected to be an estimate. This contradiction could affect legal interpretation related to delays and liabilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The agreed upon delivery dates are based on the Subcontractor's projected lead time, current inventory, commitments and Subcontractor's advice. Manufacturer agrees and acknowledges that all shipment dates are projected delivery dates. All shipments shall be F.O.B. the manufacturing facility unless otherwise mutually agreed upon in writing. The method and route of shipment shall be at Subcontractor's discretion, unless Manufacturer supplies instructions in writing at least five days prior to shipment. In addition to the purchase price, Manufacturer shall pay any and all transportation charges (including insurance). The risk of loss of and title to the Manufacturer's Products pass to Manufacturer upon the receipt of the Manufacturer's Products by the carrier. Subcontractor is not responsible for any installation of Manufacturer's Products sold hereunder or delays caused by Manufacturer-specified suppliers.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'firm delivery dates' to 'projected delivery dates' directly contradicts the understanding of delivery date guarantees within the contract. The original text implies a guarantee, while the changed text suggests an estimate. The contradicted text includes the clause "Manufacturer agrees and acknowledges that all shipment dates are projected delivery dates", which is a clear statement. The change undermines the enforceability of any deadlines, as projected dates are not binding. The original text implies that the deadlines are guaranteed and any failure to meet them will be seen as a violation of the contract, whereas the Change Text implies that deadlines can be estimates and are not guaranteed. This creates a contradiction.
